author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Shipped the atomic-claim primitive end-to-end:

- **Storage layer.** `Status::InProgress` (wire spelling
  `in-progress`, hyphenated serde rename) added between Open
  and Closed. New `Storage::claim(id, who)` and
  `Storage::unclaim(id)` methods land ONE multi-op commit with
  two trailers each (`set-assignee` + `set-status`). New
  `Error::AlreadyClaimed { by }` for different-user re-claims;
  same-user re-claim is a true no-op (no new commit lands).
- **CLI.** `jjf update --claim`/`--unclaim`, `jjf ready
  --include-claimed`, `jjf ready --claim --limit 1`. New error
  kinds wired through the JSON envelope: `already_claimed`
  (with `details.by`), `no_current_user`,
  `claim_requires_limit_one`. `Status::InProgress` accepted as
  `--status in-progress` on `update` and `ls`.
- **Spec.** Bump to v2.3 in `docs/storage-format.md` (new
  status value + new §5.9 atomic-claim shape) and
  `docs/cli-json.md` (new envelopes + error-kind rows).

**Parallel-claim race test.** Two threads racing `jjf ready
--claim --limit 1` against the same scratch repo — both
succeeded, both claimed DIFFERENT ids. The resolver/bookmark
dance handles it for free: jj's working-copy lock serializes
the two commits, and the second writer's `Storage::list_ready`
sees the first's claim already landed (InProgress is filtered
out by default) so it picks the next-priority id. No real race
surfaced — the existing storage machinery was already
race-safe by construction.

# Recommendation

Close c3cc807. The full acceptance checklist passes; 331
workspace tests green (added 12 storage + 13 CLI tests for
claim/unclaim/ready behavior).

# Confidence

High. Smoke-tested from the source tree
(`JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST=1 jjf update c3cc807 --claim` worked
end-to-end; HEAD recovered cleanly). Parallel race test
re-ran three times stably.

# Open follow-ups

- The race test's *mechanism* — does the loser see a jj
  rejection on a second `bookmark set`, or does the
  working-copy lock serialize them first? My commit message
  says the former; in practice it's the latter via jj's
  shared working copy. Not user-visible, but worth correcting
  in the spec if a future ticket cares.
- `--ttl` (auto-unclaim drift) and `jjf release` alias for
  `--unclaim` deliberately out of scope (the ticket notes).
- The `Status::InProgress` deserialize is a hard failure for
  pre-v2.3 readers crawling a v2.3 commit — older readers
  must upgrade before pulling v2.3 data. Documented in the
  v2.3 changelog.
